content: ICM curiosity-driven exploration in autonomous agents can be adapted to actively chunk scattered sensory and reasoning information into 3–5 meaningful units to fit within working memory capacity limits, rather than passively operating within unstructured memory constraints. This targeted chunking creates a self-reinforcing cycle where curiosity prioritizes high-impact information compression that preserves the most useful information for reasoning.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
